Title: MARKET PULSE PM MAY 22, 2026 [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The FBM KLCI closed higher on Friday as bargain hunting resurfaced following several weak trading sessions.
The key index rose more than four points to 1,712.
The ringgit, meanwhile, weakened against the US dollar at 3.9650.
In the cryptocurrency market, Bitcoin fell to RM307,493  as uncertainty over US-Iran negotiations weighed on broader risk sentiment.
Following the trend, Ethereum traded lower at RM8,446 while Solana stood at RM347.
That's it for Market Pulse.

Title: Seafood, water quality in Kuala Juru safe, says Fisheries Department
Article:
BUKIT MERTAJAM: Laboratory analysis of water and shellfish samples from Kuala Juru waters near here has confirmed that seafood and water quality in the area are safe and comply with national food safety standards.
The Fisheries Department said the analysis was conducted by the Fisheries Research Institute (IPP) Batu Maung in collaboration with the Kuantan Fisheries Biosecurity Centre and the Selangor Fisheries Biosecurity Centre, following complaints related to pollution from the local community.
The investigation covered heavy metals, water quality, plankton and marine biotoxins.
Tests found that heavy metal content, including mercury, cadmium and lead, in shellfish samples was below the maximum limits permitted under the Food Act 1983.
Marine biotoxin analysis using the High-Performance Liquid Chromatography (HPLC) method also recorded low and safe levels of Paralytic Shellfish Poisoning (PSP) toxins.
Microscopic analysis of water samples further confirmed the absence of toxic microalgae such as Alexandrium spp., Dinophysis spp. and Pseudo-nitzschia spp., which are known to cause the red tide phenomenon.
In addition, ammonia and nitrate readings were found to comply with the National Water Quality Standards Class IIA for the conservation of sensitive aquatic life.
Penang Fisheries director Azimah Jumatli said the findings showed that Kuala Juru waters and seafood from the area remained safe and under control.
"The public should not panic as the analysis confirms that fisheries resources in Kuala Juru are still safe for consumption and do not pose a health risk to consumers," she said today.
She said the department would continue regular monitoring of biosecurity and water quality to ensure the country's fisheries resources remain protected.
It was recently reported that about 100 shellfish farmers and fishermen in Kampung Kuala Juru near here are growing increasingly alarmed over worsening pollution in Sungai Juru, which they claimed was destroying catches and threatening livelihoods built over generations.
The
contamination
has already caused income losses of more than 50 per cent following repeated deaths of farmed shellfish and fish in the river, which locals claimed was heavily polluted by industrial effluent and wastewater from nearby aquaculture operations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: JAG back in black with RM5.37mil net profit in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: JAG Bhd returned to profitability with a net profit of RM5.37 million in the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026 compared to a net loss of RM6.43 million last year.
JAG recorded RM64.58 million revenue, representing a 39.0 per cent increase from RM46.47 million in the corresponding quarter last year.
In a statement, the total waste management (TWM) and resource recovery specialist said the improved performance was principally driven by its TWM business, which remains the core earnings engine and accounted for 97.7 per cent of total revenue during the quarter.
The segment revenue from manufacturing and trading activities increased 41.5 per cent year-on-year (YoY) to RM63.09 million from RM44.60 million.
This was mainly due to higher sales contribution from high value precious metals recovered and sold during the quarter.
"The segment also benefited from firmer market prices of key precious metals, particularly gold and silver, which supported stronger selling prices and contributed positively to profit margins," it said.
JAG declared a first interim single-tier dividend of RM0.002 per share, amounting to RM1.40 million for the financial year ending December 31, 2026.
The dividend was declared on May 22, and is payable on June 25.
Executive director Datuk Ng Meow Giak said the dividend declaration reflects confidence in the company's improving earnings trajectory, while maintaining a prudent approach to capital allocation.
Ng said the board has formalised a dividend policy framework to support a more structured and sustainable approach towards future shareholder returns, with the group targeting a dividend payout of up to 30 per cent of net profit.
"Moving forward, our focus remains on enhancing sourcing capabilities, developing and expanding new streams of income within TWM, deepening downstream recovery capabilities and strengthening the quality of our product output," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bursa Malaysia ends higher on bargain hunting
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bursa Malaysia ended higher on Friday as bargain hunting resurfaced following several weak trading sessions.
At 5pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose 4.31 points, or 0.25 per cent, to 1,712.67, from yesterday's close of 1,708.36.
The benchmark index, which opened 3.35 points higher at 1,711.71, moved in a narrow range between 1,710.30 and 1,715.71 throughout the session.
Market breadth was positive, with gainers outpacing losers 629 to 539, while 540 counters were unchanged, 1,010 untraded and 64 suspended.
Turnover rose to 3.68 billion units worth RM3.58 billion compared with 3.49 billion units worth RM3.70 billion on Thursday.
Rakuten Trade Sdn Bhd vice-president of equity research Thong Pak Leng said support for the FBM KLCI remained firm above 1,710, but investors continue to adopt a cautious stance in the near term as market volatility is likely to weigh on sentiment.
"Having said that, easing concerns in Middle East and a modest decline in Brent crude prices have improved sentiment slightly," he told Bernama.
Globally, he said major regional indices trended higher on strong performance in technology stocks, while investors remained focused on US-Iran negotiations that may help ease tensions in West Asia and stabilise oil supply flows through the Strait of Hormuz.
Among heavyweights, IHH Healthcare Bhd added four sen to RM8.96, Malayan Banking Bhd and Tenaga Nasional Bhd eased four sen each to RM11.00 and RM14.42, respectively, while Public Bank Bhd and CIMB Group Holdings Bhd were flat at RM4.77 and RM7.75, respectively.
On the most active list, SkyeChip Bhd jumped 37 sen to RM2.70, EI Power Bhd rose 7.5 sen to 62.5 sen, Tanco Holdings Bhd put on two sen to RM1.72, Zetrix AI Bhd slid 2.5 sen to 79.5 sen, while GIIB Holdings Bhd was flat at 39.5 sen.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Convicted rapist jailed for confining, assaulting teenage boy
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A convicted rapist today pleaded guilty to physically assaulting and wrongfully confining a 14-year-old boy nearly four hours inside an apartment unit last week.
The
magistrate's court here sentenced
Mohamad Fadwan Abd Muaain, 42, to nine months in jail for confining the teenager by binding his hands and feet, and an additional six months for injuring the teenager.
In a separate proceeding at the Sessions Court, Fadwan pleaded guilty to physically assaulting the boy by touching his body and private parts.
The offences were allegedly committed at the same time, date and place under Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act, which carries a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping.
Judge Rasyihah Ghazali fixed June 8 for sentencing after deputy public prosecutor K. S Rinisha Priyya did not offer bail and requested time to obtain the accused's prior records.
Fadwan was brought to court today following an eight-day remand, which expires tomorrow.
It was reported that the teenager was lured into the unit after the suspect struck up a conversation and offered him a mobile phone internet package, on the condition that the victim help him carry the items.
He was arrested on Saturday at a rented room in Kuala Lumpur.
Shah Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Ramsay Embol previously said checks revealed the suspect had been convicted of rape, wrongful confinement and robbery involving a woman 18 years ago.
He was released from prison in April last year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chery previews flagship Tiggo 9 SUV, estimated price under RM189,000
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Chery Auto Malaysia has previewed Chery Tiggo 9, the largest and most luxurious model in the Tiggo lineup so far, signalling the company's continued push into the premium family SUV segment.
Positioned as Chery's flagship SUV, the Tiggo 9 is aimed at families and modern drivers seeking more space, comfort and upscale features without venturing into luxury-brand pricing territory.
Chery Auto Malaysia vice president Lee Wen Hsiang said the Tiggo 9 represents an important step in Chery's long-term growth strategy in Malaysia as demand continues to rise for larger and more versatile vehicles.
"As families grow and lifestyles evolve, there is a clear demand for more space, more versatility and greater comfort and this is exactly what the Tiggo 9 delivers," he said.
The seven-seater SUV combines a bold exterior design with premium touches throughout the cabin.
Among the standout styling elements are the large 3D front grille, LED headlights with sequential indicators and 20-inch alloy wheels, giving the vehicle a more commanding road presence.
On the interior, the Tiggo 9 features leather seats, ventilated seating for both front and second-row passengers, massage seats for the front-row occupants, ambient lighting and a panoramic sunroof designed to enhance long-distance travel comfort.
The SUV also comes equipped with a large infotainment display supporting Android Auto and Apple CarPlay, a Sony sound system, wireless charging with a cooling function and a head-up display.
Beyond comfort features, the Tiggo 9 also places strong emphasis on safety and driving assistance technologies, equipped with 10 airbags, an intelligent all-wheel drive system and a comprehensive ADAS 2.5 suite featuring 23 functions such as adaptive cruise control, lane keep assist and autonomous emergency braking.
The Tiggo 9 is also the first model in the Tiggo series to feature Auto Park Assist, aimed at making urban driving and parking more convenient.
Chery hinted at future plans to introduce more advanced powertrain options for the Tiggo 9, including a plug-in hybrid electric vehicle (PHEV) variant.
The estimated selling price for the Tiggo 9 is below RM189,000.
Buyers will also receive a seven-year warranty package or coverage of up to 150,000km, whichever comes first.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gentari ramps up safety focus at Kedah solar project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Gentari has reaffirmed its commitment to safety and disciplined project execution as construction progresses on its 150MWp large-scale solar 5 (LSS5) project in Sungai Petani, Kedah.
The company's senior leadership team recently conducted a site safety visit to the project, underscoring its focus on responsible delivery and adherence to environmental and operational standards during the construction phase.
The project is being undertaken by Gentari LSS Lima Sdn Bhd, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Gentari Renewables Sdn Bhd, following its successful bid under the LSS5 in December 2024.
LSS5 is part of Malaysia's broader push to expand renewable energy capacity under the National Energy Transition Roadmap (NETR).
With a national target capacity of about 2,000MWac, the programme is the largest large-scale solar quota introduced in the country to date and forms a key pillar of Malaysia's transition towards cleaner energy.
The NETR aims for renewable energy to account for 70 per cent of the country's generation capacity by 2050.
Once completed, Gentari's solar photovoltaic facility is expected to generate up to 150MWp of electricity for the national grid under a 21-year power purchase agreement (PPA).
Gentari group chief operating officer and country head of Malaysia Shah Yang Razalli said the project marks a significant step from planning into execution.
"This transition from planning into execution reflects Gentari's commitment to deliver utility-scale renewable energy infrastructure that supports Malaysia's long-term energy transition.
"This project aims to build resilient, future-ready energy assets that strengthen national energy security and accelerate decarbonisation under the NETR," he said.
He added that the company's immediate priority is ensuring the project is completed according to schedule and within scope and budget while maintaining high technical, environmental and safety standards.
The project is expected to reduce Malaysia's dependence on fossil fuels and lower carbon emissions by an estimated 166,766 tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent annually once operational.
Gentari said the project would support sustainable growth in the Northern Corridor Economic Region.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Massive highway jams as school holiday, festive exodus begin [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Motorists faced widespread congestion across major highways in the Klang Valley and beyond this afternoon, with several accidents and lane closures adding to the evening rush hour buildup.
"Three accidents were reported within the space of an hour," the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) said in a traffic update on its Whatsapp channel.
At 3.53pm, it said that a crash at Km18.5 of the New Klang Valley Expressway southbound blocked the middle and right lanes, causing a 6.4km crawl between Kota Damansara and Damansara.
Minutes later, LLM said that a collision on the Sprint Expressway's Damansara Link eastbound obstructed the left lane near Km2.5.
At 4.15pm, it reported that a crash on the East Coast Expressway 1 eastbound near Sri Jaya blocked the emergency lane, though traffic remained manageable.
Heavy traffic was reported on multiple expressways, with notable slowdowns on the Damansara-Puchong Expressway in both directions, the Shah Alam Expressway eastbound toward Subang Jaya and Sunway, and the ELITE Highway between Putra Heights and Seafield.
The North-South Expressway southbound was further disrupted by a trailer fire between Nilai and Bandar Ainsdale, LLM added.
On the North-South Expressway, congestion stretched across multiple stretches in Penang and Perak, including Taiping Utara to Bukit Merah due to maintenance works.
Heavy traffic was also reported on the Penang Bridge eastbound and at the Tambak Johor causeway toward Woodlands.
On Wednesday, LLM said it expects more than 2.2 million vehicles daily across its highway network during the upcoming back-to-back holiday period starting Friday.
For the East Coast Expressway Phase 2 (LPT2), traffic is projected to reach up to 50,000 vehicles per day during peak periods.
LLM said the surge was driven by the convergence of the mid-year school holidays with three major celebrations – Hari Raya Aidiladha, Wesak Day and the Official Birthday of His Majesty the King.
Peak departure days from the Klang Valley are expected on May 22, 23, 26, 28, 29, 30 and 31, while return traffic is forecast to peak on June 4, 5, 6 and 7.
Motorists can stay informed by following real-time traffic updates on LLM's official social media channels.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Teen killed in crash had just been accepted into polytechnic
Article:
KOTA BARU: A grieving father said his 18-year-old son, killed in a horrific crash in Kuala Terengganu on Tuesday, had just received news of his acceptance into a polytechnic - a dream he had long waited for.
Wah Tee Tun, 49, said the family discovered today that Han Keong had been accepted into Politeknik Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin to pursue a Diploma in Mechanical Engineering (Automotive).
"This morning, I checked his application through the Central University Admissions Unit (UPU).
"It stated that my son had been accepted to continue his studies at Politeknik Sultan Mizan Zainal Abidin.
"He had always wanted to further his studies at the polytechnic. It was something he had been waiting for all this while," said the father of two.
Tee Tun said his son's body was brought to Kelantan yesterday for cremation at the Ariyakiri Buddhist Temple in Bachok.
"The cremation process was completed at about 6pm yesterday.
"Today, we brought Han Keong's ashes to Wat Kampung Tanggol in Terengganu for final prayers and religious rites," he added.
The teenager was killed in a horrific
crash
in Manir, Kuala Terengganu, when an electricity pole crashed through the front windscreen of the van he and his father were travelling in.
The incident also left his father traumatised after witnessing the fatal injuries suffered by his son during the crash.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Construction momentum lifts MRCB's Q1 results
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Resources Corp Bhd (MRCB) posted a 46 per cent increase in revenue to RM319.2 million and an 87 per cent growth in pre-tax profit to RM9.1 million in the first quarter of 2026.
The stronger performance was driven by improved contributions from the engineering, construction and environment (ECE) division, largely from the recently completed LRT3 project and higher revenues from the Kompleks Sukan Shah Alam redevelopment project.
MRCB said its construction revenues will grow further in 2026, with the development of the five reinstated LRT3 stations and other related infrastructure works, which will commence in the current quarter.
The property development and investment division recorded revenue of RM40.8 million and an operating loss of RM8.9 million in Q1 2026.
The lower revenue and higher operating losses were primarily due to reduced contributions from completed developments namely Sentral Suites, VIVO 9 Seputeh, TRIA 9 Seputeh and Alstonia in Bukit Rahman Putra, where inventories of completed units available for sale continued to deplete.
The division is targeting property sales of RM800 million in 2026, split equally between developments in Malaysia and its international projects, namely Maris and Vista in Gold Coast, Australia, and The Symphony Centre in New Zealand.
In Malaysia, the group has RM2.2 billion in property launches planned for 2026, subject to obtaining the necessary approvals.
The ECE division recorded revenue of RM257.9 million in Q1 2026, representing a 69 per cent increase from the corresponding period in 2025.
The division's operating profit rose more than threefold to RM38.6 million. Revenue contributions were mainly derived from the LRT3 project, which achieved overall physical and financial progress of 99.8 per cent and 99.9 per cent respectively, and the redevelopment of Kompleks Sukan Shah Alam.
Other contributions came from the Muara Sungai Pahang Phase 3 and Sungai Langat Phase 2 flood mitigation projects, and the North-South Expressway Phase 1 highway widening project.
MRCB said contributions from the reinstatement of five LRT3 stations and other related infrastructure works remained minimal during the quarter.
"However, following receipt of the notice to proceed with the project, works will shortly begin, and revenue recognition will accelerate as construction activities gain momentum."
The division continues to focus on executing its order book, including about RM5.5 billion in projects secured in 2025, which are expected to provide stronger earnings visibility as execution activities accelerate.
The division also maintains an active open tender book of RM10.0 billion across various transportation infrastructure, water and environmental-related works and public facility projects.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Explosion at MOL's Tiszaujvaros petrochemical plant kills one, injures several
Article:
BUDAPEST: An explosion at Hungarian oil and gas group MOL's petrochemical plant in Tiszaujvaros, eastern Hungary, has killed one person and seriously injured several others, the company said in a statement on Friday.
Prime Minister Peter Magyar said in a Facebook post that his economy minister Istvan Kapitany and MOL executive chairman Zsolt Hernadi were en route to the plant. Magyar also posted a photo that showed huge black smoke billowing out of the plant.
"An explosion occurred at MOL Petrochemicals' site in Tiszaujvaros during the restart of the Olefin 1 plant. The fire was localised by firefighters, and the intervention is still ongoing," MOL said.
"The circumstances of the accident are being investigated by experts," the company added, without giving further details.
Kapitany, in a Facebook post, said that according to current information, a compressor exploded during the restart of the Olefin 1 plant and the fire was still being put out.
The Olefin 1 plant is a steam cracker unit at MOL's petrochemical plant in Tiszaujvaros. The production capacity of MOL's Olefin-1 plant is approximately 370,000 metric tons of ethylene per year. There are two steam crackers in Tiszaujvaros with 660 kt/y ethylene capacity according to MOL's website.
MOL uses the majority of its ethylene internally to manufacture polyethylene plastics, which are sold to the plastics and packaging industries.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Subang MP raises concern over sudden MyKhas Portal access denial
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Subang member of parliament Wong Chen has alleged that his office was denied access to the MyKhas Portal without prior any notice, thereby affecting applications for allocations for the constituency.
"I denounce the sudden and unnotified denial of access to my parliamentary office's MyKhas Portal account," he said in a statement posted on his Facebook page.
He said the restriction has disrupted constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat applications for Subang residents.
The portal, managed by the Implementation Coordination Unit under the Prime Minister's Department, is used to process constituency allocations and Projek Mesra Rakyat applications.
The disruption was discovered on May 20 when his officer attempted to upload Projek Mesra Rakyat funding applications for schools, only to be told verbally by a civil servant that access had been blocked on instructions from superiors in Putrajaya.
Wong's office emailed the Implementation Coordination Unit the same day and hand-delivered official letters to both the Selangor branch and headquarters in Putrajaya on May 21.
Both offices acknowledged receipt.
As of 10am today, no access had been restored and no written explanation had been provided.
A cross-check with other PKR MPs in Selangor revealed their accounts remained fully operational, prompting Wong to question whether his office was being selectively targeted.
"This raises serious questions as to why the Subang parliamentary office is the only constituency being targeted and denied access.
"If selective treatment has indeed taken place, it is deeply regrettable and directly contradicts the Madani principles of a fair, transparent and professional administration," he added.
He said the Implementation Coordination Unit Selangor had long recognised his office as a model parliamentary office, and that his team had publicly published its community allocation accounts every six months for over a decade.
"Denying us access without notice or justification not only disrupts essential welfare initiatives for the people of Subang, but also unfairly tarnishes the excellent reputation of my administrative staff," he said.
He is demanding that the Prime Minister's Department immediately restore access and issue a formal written explanation at the earliest possible opportunity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lebanon says Israeli strike kills six, including two rescuers and child
Article:
BEIRUT: An Israeli strike on southern Lebanon killed six people on Friday, including two rescuers and a child, the Lebanese health ministry said, despite a fragile ceasefire in the Israel-Hizbollah war.
In a statement, the ministry said that "six people were martyred" including two rescuers from the Risala Scouts association, one of them also working as a freelance photographer, and a Syrian girl in a strike on Deir Qanun al-Nahr village. The association is linked with the Hizbollah-allied Amal movement.
An earlier strike on the southern town of Hanaway on Friday also killed four rescuers from the Hizbollah-linked Islamic Health Committee, the ministry said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tow truck driver charged with fracturing wife's arm with chair, obstructing policeman
Article:
MELAKA: A tow truck driver pleaded not guilty at the Ayer Keroh Sessions Court today to a charge of causing grievous hurt to his wife by throwing a vanity table chair at her earlier this month.
Muhammad Rizman Akmal Ahmadiah, 28, claimed trial after the charge was read to him before Judge Raja Noor Adilla Raja Mahyaldin.
He was accused of causing grievous hurt to his wife, Syaudah Ahmad, 27, by throwing a vanity table chair at her, resulting in a fractured left arm.
The injury was confirmed by medical officer Dr Durratul Ain Shohaimi when the victim received treatment at Sultanah Fatimah Specialist Hospital in Muar, Johor.
The offence was allegedly committed at a house in Kampung Seri Mawar, Paya Redan, Tiang Dua here at 6.30am on May 6.
The charge was framed under Section 325 of the Penal Code, read together with Section 326A of the same Code, which carries a maximum prison sentence of 14 years and a fine upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Rifah Izatti Abdul Mutalif proposed bail at RM15,000, with additional conditions that the accused is prohibited from approaching or harassing the victim until the completion of the trial.
Defence counsel Fahmy Sulaiman pleaded for a lower bail amount, citing that the accused supports his wife, their one-year-old child, an unemployed mother, and a father suffering from heart disease and high cholesterol.
He added that the accused, who works as a tow truck driver, an insurance runner, and a food delivery rider, also supports two younger siblings who are still studying.
"The accused turned himself in at the Melaka contingent police headquarters on May 11, demonstrating that he is not a flight risk and has cooperated fully with the investigating officer," Fahmy said.
In a separate proceeding at the magistrate's court, the accused also pleaded not guilty to a charge of using criminal force to deter a public servant from discharging his duty.
He was charged with scuffling with Sergeant Faizanizam Amir Fadzil to obstruct the police officer from executing his lawful duties at the same location at 11.45am on May 8.
The charge was framed under Section 353 of the Penal Code, which provides for a jail term of up to two years, a fine, or both, upon conviction.
The case was heard before magistrate Sharda Shienha Mohd Suleiman.
Deputy public prosecutor Shaza Nur Sharif requested bail at RM8,000, which Fahmy appealed using the same mitigation grounds.
The Sessions Court granted bail at RM8,000 with one surety for the first case, alongside the additional conditions requested by the prosecution, while the Magistrate's Court fixed bail at RM3,500 for the second charge.
June 30 was set for mention of both cases for the submission of documents.
NST, News, Malaysia News, Region, tow truck driver, grievous hurt, wife, vanity chair, fractured arm, Ayer Keroh Sessions Court, magistrate's court, Melaka
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Analysts: Inclusive representation the way forward for Bersama to rival DAP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Just days after former ministers Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli and Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad announced a political
"kamikaze"
move, the revitalised Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) has emerged as a possible force to reshape Malaysia's political landscape.
The recently-acquired party has seen over 8,000 new members in less than 24 hours following its launch on Sunday, according to Rafizi's Instagram announcement on Monday.
The party's entry also raises questions about its preparedness to attract voters as election rumours build up.
Sharing similar appeals to Pakatan Harapan's DAP, Bersama stands out as a pro-reformist party, raising issues ranging from raising government aid for households and advancing fair wages, outlined in their 12-point manifesto.
"They do have similarities in terms of progressive policy agenda like institutional reforms and good governance.
"While both also champion multiethnic inclusion, DAP has a stronger bond with the non-Muslim communities," Sunway University political scientist Professor Wong Chin Huat told the New Straits Times.
Meanwhile, University of Nottingham Malaysia political analyst Dr Bridget Welsh considers ethnic factors a matter of importance that defines the future of Bersama.
"The reality is that ethnic factors, in terms of representation do matter," she said.
While it is still too early to anticipate the potential strategies of the PKR splinter party, its newly unveiled 12-point agenda has drawn nationwide scrutiny, raising questions over the party's commitment to merit-based decision-making.
"They will likely paint themselves as 'new' and 'different' to appeal to fence sitters, especially pro-reform voters, part of the PH base and youth.
"Strategically, I expect they will work to secure candidates, including those from PKR aligned to them," Bridget added.
Wong also highlighted two things for the party to consider in order to demonstrate their commitment against corruption and become a comparable alternative to DAP.
"For Bersama to wrest votes and seats from DAP, Bersama has to firstly show a clearer solidarity with the minority communities.
"Secondly, they need to convince voters they can carry their constituencies and not split PH's votes to let PN or BN win," he said.
Following the party's surge of 8,000 new registrations on Monday, the duo also announced they would not seek top party posts, underscoring their commitment to integrity and an uncompromising stance against graft and corruption.
However, whether it can sustain a high level of principled conduct remains uncertain.
"It doesn't matter much if the party's power is eventually held by MPs, like what British parties used to be.
"For Bersama to stay democratic, its candidate selection must be bottom up, by members or voters, rather than party leaders," Wong said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China squeezes Japan over rare earths in repeat of 2010 showdown
Article:
SINGAPORE: China has cut Japan off from several heavy rare earths and other materials for at least four months, coinciding with a dispute between the two countries over Taiwan, suggesting Beijing is using its control over critical minerals as diplomatic leverage.
Japan is the largest rare earth magnet maker outside China but, like the rest of the world, is overwhelmingly dependent on Beijing for imports of certain so-called heavy rare earths used in magnet-making, aerospace and defence, as well as gallium, a minor metal vital for chip-making.
Since December, Chinese exports of rare earth minerals like dysprosium, terbium and yttrium oxide, as well as speciality metal gallium, to Japan have stopped except for a few tiny shipments of yttrium, Chinese customs data shows.
Major Japanese magnet maker Shin-Etsu has stopped accepting new orders for dysprosium-containing magnets, according to a Western customer who spoke on condition of anonymity. The company declined to comment.
The halt to exports, which began shortly after a diplomatic row over Taiwan erupted in November, is similar to Beijing's throttling of exports of such materials to the US during the current trade war.
Beijing publicly tightened export controls to Japan in January, and then twice again the following month, targeting major conglomerates including the shipbuilding and aero engine divisions of Mitsubishi Heavy Industries.
Japan's trade minister Ryosei Akazawa, who is currently in China, is scheduled to attend meetings on Saturday. He is the most senior Japanese official to visit China since the dispute erupted.
Tokyo is taking measures such as releasing stockpiled supplies where necessary although it does not disclose details, said an official at the Japanese industry ministry, adding that the government is aware of concerns over rising prices and tightening supplies.
RARE EARTH DEJA VU
Japanese companies are better insulated from the pressure campaign after a similar slowdown in Chinese exports of rare earth minerals in 2010 prompted the building of stockpiles, said David Merriman, research director at Project Blue. They have also sought to curb usage of heavy rare earths in magnets and look for alternatives.
China continues to export normal quantities of the finished rare earth magnets used by the automotive industry and other industrial companies, according to data.
Components manufacturer TDK told Reuters it currently does not expect any major impact and is diversifying its supply sources. Mitsubishi Motors said in February it had secured rare earths until mid-year.
Japan has helped fund alternative producers such as Australia-based Lynas Rare Earths, which last year became the first commercial producer of separated terbium and dysprosium outside China. It has also launched rare earth projects in Australia and France and a gallium project in Australia.
However, it is likely to take years to replace Chinese supply for heavy rare earths. In the first quarter of 2026, Lynas produced eight metric tons of dysprosium and terbium. China exported about 14 tons a month of the two minerals to Japan in 2024.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Citi to boost Asia wealth business with 'significant' hires, global head says
Article:
HONG KONG: Citigroup plans to allocate a significant portion of its global wealth management hiring to Asia, where its private bank is growing faster and generating higher productivity than in other regions, the bank's global wealth head Andy Sieg said.
The US bank's recently unveiled hiring plans would be "anchored" in Asia along with other regions, said Sieg, who formerly led Merrill Lynch's wealth business and was brought in by Citi CEO Jane Fraser in 2023 to lead a revamp of the wealth unit.
Citi plans to hire about 100 private bankers globally, alongside roughly 400 other specialists, as part of a broader effort to lift returns in its wealth business, he said at the bank's investor day event earlier this month.
"In the private bank, our business in Asia is the fastest growing part of our private bank," Sieg said in an interview in Hong Kong. "It's the most productive area of the private bank."
He declined to elaborate on the hiring plan in the region, but said "a significant percentage of the hiring will be here in Asia, you know, commensurate with the fact that this is a large percentage of our global business."
Citi this month set a target of return on tangible common equity for the wealth unit of 15 per cent to 20 per cent in 2027 and 2028 and above 20 per cent over the medium term. The wealth unit delivered a net income rise of nearly 50 per cent to US$1.5 billion in 2025 from a year earlier.
Asia is a key pillar of that strategy, Sieg said.
The bank's Asia wealth business, including Japan, Asia North and Australia and Asia South, generated about $US3 billion in revenue in 2025, or about 35 per cent of Citi's global wealth revenue, the bank's latest official filings show.
Sieg said Indonesia was a good example of how Citi can support wealthy clients during periods of market and policy uncertainty.
"It's also complex right now," he said. "Markets have been volatile, political and policy changes being announced every few days."
Citi has retained its wealth, cards and retail banking operations in Hong Kong and Singapore, even as it moved in recent years to exit consumer banking in 14 markets across Asia, Europe, the Middle East and Mexico as part of Fraser's strategy to simplify the firm and focus capital on higher-return businesses.
The bank is seeking to increase income from existing clients, having merged retail banking into the wealth unit in the US in the first quarter.
"Jane and the board, they will not be satisfied with a business which is only marginally advanced from where we are today," Sieg said.
"They expect us to build an industry leader in wealth management."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Debt collector jailed 15 months for confining, injuring teenager
Article:
SHAH ALAM: A debt collector was sentenced to 15 months' jail by the magistrate's vourt today after pleading guilty to wrongfully confining a 14-year-old boy for nearly four hours and injuring him inside an apartment unit last week.
Magistrate Wan Noora Nisa Ngadirin handed down the sentence to Mohamad Fadwan Abd Muaain, 42, after the charges were read to him.
Fadwan appeared in court handcuffed and dressed in a black and red striped collared T-shirt and jeans.
The court ordered the divorcee to serve nine months for confining the boy by binding his hands and feet, and an additional six months for injuring the teenager.
Both offences were committed between 8.05am and 11.50am at an apartment unit in Section 32 on May 11.
The court ordered the sentences to run consecutively from today.
The charges were framed under Sections 342 and 323 of the Penal Code, which carry a maximum one-year jail term, a fine of up to RM2,000, or both upon conviction.
Deputy public prosecutor Nur Amirah Zahari urged the court to impose a proportionate sentence reflecting the gravity of harming a child.
In mitigation, the National Legal Aid Foundation requested a lenient custodial sentence, noting that the accused expressed remorse and had cooperated with the police.
Before sentencing, the magistrate twice questioned Fadwan over his actions.
"Why did you confine and injure the boy? You know he is a child, and you are 42. Did you think of the consequences? she asked.
He replied that he wanted to break into the boy's house to "retrieve gold."
His unclear response prompted the magistrate to repeat the question, but he remained silent.
The court heard that the police rescued the boy from the unit where he was found half-naked with his hands and feet bound. The boy claimed to have been sexually assaulted.
The teenager told police that he had been threatened not to leave and was punched, kicked and beaten with a heavy object.
In a separate proceeding before Sessions Court judge Rasyihah Ghazali, Fadwan pleaded guilty to physically assaulting the same boy by touching his body and private parts.
The offence was allegedly committed at the same time, date and place under Section 14(d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act, which carries a maximum 20-year jail term and whipping.
Rasyihah fixed June 8 for sentencing after deputy public prosecutor K. S Rinisha Priyya did not offer bail and requested time to obtain the accused's prior records.
Fadwan was brought to court today following an eight-day remand, which expires tomorrow.
It was reported that the teenager was lured into the unit after the suspect struck up a conversation and offered him a mobile phone internet package, on the condition that the victim help him carry the items.
Shah Alam police chief Assistant Commissioner Ramsay Embol previously said checks revealed the suspect had also been convicted of rape, wrongful confinement and robbery involving a woman 18 years ago.
The suspect, who was released from prison in April last year, was arrested on Saturday at a rented room in Kuala Lumpur.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Negara reserves at US$129bil as at May 15
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Negara Malaysia's international reserves stood at US$129.5 billion as at May 15, 2026.
"The reserves position is sufficient to finance 4.6 months of imports of goods and services, and is 0.9 times the total short-term external debt," it said in a statement.
According to Bank Negara, the gross international reserves comprise foreign currency reserves (US$113.5 billion), International Monetary Fund (IMF) reserves position (US$1.3 billion), special drawing rights (US$5.9 billion), gold (US$6.4 billion) and other reserve assets (US$2.4 billion).
Meanwhile, the central bank said the total assets amounted to RM610.32 billion.
This includes mainly gold, foreign financial assets and other reserves including special drawing rights (RM522.55 billion), Malaysian government papers (RM13.10 billion), deposits with financial institutions (RM1.097 billion), loans and advances (RM28.92 billion), property and equipment (RM4.55 billion) and other assets (RM40.10 billion).
Capital and liabilities totalled RM610.32 billion, including paid-up capital (RM100 million) and reserves (RM196.22 billion), as well as currency in circulation (RM183.66 billion).
Other components include deposits by financial institutions at RM112.58 billion, while federal government deposits stood at RM9.52 billion and other deposits at RM70.51 billion.
Bank Negara papers totalled RM7.59 billion, alongside allocation of special drawing rights (RM26.38 billion) and other liabilities (RM3.76 billion).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: US navy official says Taiwan arms sales on 'pause' over Iran war
Article:
WASHINGTON: The acting US Navy secretary said Thursday that arms sales to Taiwan had been put on "pause" to ensure that the American military had sufficient munitions for its Iran operations.
Asked at a congressional hearing about the stalled US$14 billion weapons purchase by Taiwan, acting Secretary Hung Cao said that "right now we're doing a pause in order to make sure we have the munitions we need for Epic Fury — which we have plenty."
"But, we're just making sure we have everything, then the foreign military sales will continue when the administration deems necessary."
The US State Department and the Pentagon did not immediately respond to requests for comment on Cao's remarks.
Taiwan's presidential office spokeswoman Karen Kuo said Friday there was "no information indicating that the US intends to make any adjustments to this arms sale."
A Chinese foreign ministry spokesman told a news conference Friday that Beijing's "position of resolute opposition to US arms sales to China's Taiwan region is consistent, clear and firm."
US President Donald Trump has not committed to following through with the sale, raising concerns over his commitment to support for Taiwan.
The United States recognises only Beijing, but under US law is required to provide weapons to the self-ruled democracy for its defence.
China has sworn to take the island and has not ruled out using force, ramping up military pressure in recent years.
Ahead of his visit last week to China, Trump said he would speak with Chinese President Xi Jinping about the arms sales, a departure from Washington's previous insistence that it would not consult Beijing on the matter.
After the visit, Trump said he made no commitments to Xi about Taiwan and would be making a determination on the arms sales "over the next fairly short period of time."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Prisons Dept announces special Kaamatan, Gawai visits for inmates' families
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Prisons Department has announced special family visits and meetings for inmates in conjunction with the Kaamatan Festival in Sabah and the Gawai Dayak celebration in Sarawak.
The department said the special visits would involve all prison institutions, including Moral Rehabilitation Centres, Special Rehabilitation Centres, Special Detention Facilities and Henry Gurney Schools.
"For Sabah, visits in all prison institutions statewide have been scheduled for June 4 and 5, while visits in Sarawak prisons will be held on June 5 and 6.
"The department said face-to-face visits would be conducted from 8.15am to 4.15pm daily. Visitors are required to bring their identity cards and visitor passes to facilitate the process," it said in a statement today.
It said visitors are also prohibited from bringing food and drinks for inmates, although such items can be purchased from prison canteens.
"The public is also discouraged from giving money directly to inmates, as all necessities are already provided by the department.
"However, if visitors wish to provide funds for specific matters such as examination fees, they are advised to obtain an official PPT.28 receipt as proof of payment," the department said.
It added that all visitors would be subject to prison laws and regulations, including physical inspections upon entering and leaving prison premises.
Meanwhile, online meetings will also be conducted over three days from June 7 to 9 to allow family members unable to attend in person to communicate with inmates. Families have been advised to contact the respective prison institutions for further information.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar hails Indah Water's global award for solar-powered sewage project
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said that the international recognition received by Indah Water Konsortium (IWK) reflects the country's commitment to strengthening sustainable water and sanitation management.
Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said that efforts to reinforce this aspect align with the 2030 Sustainable Development Agenda (SDGs).
"May this success continue to inspire efforts to build a more sustainable, inclusive, and prosperous nation for future generations," he said on Facebook.
IWK won the Sustainable Development Goal 6 (SDG 6) Award category at the 2026 Global Water Awards in Madrid, Spain, last Tuesday.
The award, presented by Global Water Intelligence (GWI), recognises the company's submission titled "Solar-powered Sanitation: No Upfront Cost, Cuts Emissions."
The project demonstrates how the utilisation of renewable energy can enhance the operational performance of wastewater treatment, while driving sustainable infrastructure development and strengthening financial resilience.
IWK in a statement, said that the award-winning programme involves the implementation of solar energy systems across 16 sewage treatment plants nationwide through a Private Finance Initiative (PFI) model.
This initiative generates a solar energy capacity of 6.3 MWp and produces approximately 7,700 MWh of renewable energy annually to support the treatment of nearly 148 million m3 of wastewater each year.
"This initiative is expected to save approximately RM418 million in grid electricity costs, while reducing more than 5 Gg of carbon dioxide emissions annually," the statement read.
IWK chief executive officer, Narendran Maniam, said that sustainability is at the core of every aspect of the company's operations.
He added that this initiative reflects IWK's ongoing commitment to strengthening sustainability efforts, not only from an environmental perspective but also from a financial standpoint.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bersatu to discuss Hadi's comment on ties with Pas in leadership meetings
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu will raise issues over Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang's recent remarks on ties between the two parties at its upcoming political bureau and supreme leadership council meetings.
Bersatu information chief Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz said the party viewed Hadi's statement on cooperation between Bersatu and Pas within Perikatan Nasional seriously.
"Bersatu is of the view that every matter raised should be examined comprehensively in the spirit of camaraderie, mutual understanding and shared responsibility to strengthen Perikatan Nasional's (PN) unity," he said in a statement today.
He said the party would scrutinise every issue raised and discuss them in depth at the Bersatu political bureau meeting and supreme leadership council meeting later.
Earlier, Hadi said Pas would
re-evaluate its cooperation with Bersatu
following several actions by the party that allegedly violated the spirit of camaraderie within PN.
He said the decision was made following a series of actions by Bersatu that had displeased the party.
Most recently, he said, Bersatu sacked several of its leaders from their positions, including Kedah state executive councillors and Kelantan state legislative assembly deputy speaker Datuk Mohamed Farid Mohamed Zawawi.
He said the replacement or dismissal of any member should not be done arbitrarily, but must be based on solid reasons that affect a party's performance.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gate closure improves punctuality but not attendance, says NUTP
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A strict school gate closure policy introduced by a Melaka school may improve punctuality but is unlikely to resolve absenteeism, the National Union of the Teaching Profession (NUTP)  said.
The principal of SMK Sungai Rambai recently came under public scrutiny
after enforcing a 7.30am gate closure
aimed at curbing truancy and poor attendance, prompting criticism and the appearance of a protest banner near the school.
NUTP secretary-general Fouzi Singon said punctuality and attendance are two separate matters and should not be treated as the same.
"Arriving late is a matter of discipline and punctuality, but it cannot be equated with absenteeism, which is a separate issue," he said.
He said while such measures may encourage discipline and prepare students for working life, they do not necessarily translate into better attendance.
Fouzi, a former senior assistant for student affairs, said he had previously implemented similar measures but found a rigid 7.30am cut-off excessive.
He said some students were late due to family circumstances, including reliance on guardians who leave for work later in the morning.
He added schools should first issue reminders and warnings to latecomers before taking stricter action.
"What I did previously was issue warnings several times and record the students' names.
"If the problem persisted, we would contact parents or guardians before considering stricter measures," he said.
Fouzi also stressed that parental cooperation was crucial in addressing poor attendance.
"When attendance is low, it reflects the need for stronger cooperation between schools and parents to ensure students attend consistently," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLIA taxi tout caught offering RM109 ride to Sungai Long
Article:
SEPANG: A taxi tout operating at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 was detained by the Road Transport Department (RTD) yesterday.
Selangor RTD deputy director Datuk Ahmad Kamarunzaman Mehat said the man had offered transport services using a Toyota Innova, which was subsequently seized.
The tout had approached and offered transport services to three Malaysian passengers from KLIA Terminal 1 to Sungai Long, Kajang, for RM109, payable upon arrival.
"Those found guilty may face action under Section 205(1) of the Land Public Transport Act, which provides for a fine of up to RM50,000, jail of up to five years, or both, upon conviction.
"Those found abetting illegal touting activities may also be charged under Section 243(1) of the same Act.
"The public is advised to use only legitimate transport services, either through service counters at KLIA Terminals 1 and 2 or by booking via e-hailing applications," he said in a statement today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Insya-Allah sufficient': PM on Malaysia's energy supply amid Middle East tensions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has assured that the country's energy supply will remain sufficient despite concerns arising from the ongoing conflict in the Middle East.
"Insya-Allah, Insya-Allah, it will be sufficient," he told reporters briefly when asked today.
However, Anwar, who is also finance minister, did not elaborate on the government's measures to ensure continued energy security.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof had earlier said the government was working to ensure the country's
energy supply
could be sustained until December following disruptions from the Middle East conflict.
The energy transition and water transformation minister said Malaysia's current energy supply was expected to remain sufficient until July, with efforts under way to secure additional sources from abroad.
Anwar had previously said Malaysia's energy supply was expected to remain sufficient at least until June.
He said that although Malaysia is an energy-producing country, it also remains a
net importer
as the products it exports are of premium quality and higher value.
Earlier, Anwar joined about 1,500 congregants in performing Friday prayers at Masjid Al-Muttaqin in Wangsa Maju here.
Clad in a light purple Baju Melayu paired with a black songkok, he arrived at the mosque about 1.30pm and was received by mosque chairman Mursyidi Abd Hamid, committee members and the congregation.
Also present were Wangsa Maju member of parliament Zahir Hassan and Setiawangsa PKR division chief Datuk Afdlin Shauki.
The Friday sermon, titled "Arafah: The Awakening of the Soul", was delivered by the mosque imam, Ustaz Shafik, who also led the prayers.
The sermon focused on the spiritual significance of the Day of Arafah and called on Muslims to strengthen unity, self-reflection and compassion within society.
Following the prayers, Anwar spent time greeting the congregation and members of the public, with many taking the opportunity to shake hands and take photographs with the prime minister before he left the mosque compound.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Do not normalise remarks seen as belittling Royal Institution, says MHO
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Statements perceived as belittling or questioning the Royal Institution should not be normalised in the country's political discourse.
Malaysian International Humanitarian Organisation (MHO) public relations chief Daniel Khoo said all parties, including politicians, should exercise greater caution when making remarks concerning the Malay rulers, as the matter involves public sensitivities and national harmony.
He said Malaysians have long witnessed how the Malay rulers consistently care for the welfare of the people and the stability of the country.
"We can see for ourselves how the ruler in my home state of Pahang, including the Tengku Mahkota of Pahang, constantly goes to the ground to observe the people's situation, listen to community problems and assist the rakyat regardless of race.
"That is why I feel it is inappropriate for any party, including politicians, to issue statements touching on the powers of the Malay rulers in a manner seen as diminishing the royal institution," he said when contacted today.
He said although Malaysia practises a constitutional monarchy system, this should not be used as a reason to belittle or undermine the position of the Malay rulers.
"Just because their powers are constitutionally limited does not mean the royal institution should be looked down upon.
"The Malay rulers still hold a very important position in the country and continue to command the respect of the people," he said.
[[nid:1445845]]
Khoo said such remarks should not be treated as commonplace or normalised, as they could affect social harmony.
"We live in a multiracial and multi-religious country. That is why everyone must observe proper decorum and sensitivity when speaking about the Malay rulers.
"Do not make statements like these a norm in the country's political landscape because the people still place high respect on the royal institution," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Prison inmates allowed family visits for Hari Raya Aidiladha
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Prison inmates will be allowed special family visits in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidiladha on May 29 and 30.
The Prisons Department said the visits, fixed for the third and fourth day of Aidiladha, will involve all prisons, including moral rehabilitation centres, special rehabilitation centres, special detention centres and Henry Gurney schools.
"Visiting hours will be from 8.15am to 4.15pm.
"Visitors are required to bring their identification cards and visiting cards to facilitate the process," it said in a statement today.
The department said visitors are not allowed to bring food or drinks for inmates, adding that these items can be purchased from prison canteens.
Priority for visits will be given to Muslim inmates.
"Visitors are not encouraged to give money to inmates as all basic facilities have been provided by the Prisons Department.
"However, if visitors wish to give money for specific purposes such as examination fees, they must obtain the official PPT.28 receipt as proof of acknowledgement," it said.
The department said visitors are also subject to the Prison Act and regulations, which allow prison authorities to conduct physical checks when they enter and leave prison premises.
Online meetings will also be held for three days from the fourth to sixth day of Aidiladha, from May 30 to June 1, for family members who are unable to attend in person.
Families have been advised to contact the relevant prison institution for further details.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Therapist fined RM4,000 for spreading false Budi Madani RON95 information on Facebook
Article:
SEPANG: An alternative treatment therapist was fined RM4,000 by the Sessions Court today after pleading guilty to spreading false information regarding the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) initiative on Facebook.
Judge Ahmad Fuad Othman handed down the sentence to Ariffaizal Mohd Nor Ariffin, 46.
The father of nine faces six months in jail if he fails to pay the fine.
He was charged with knowingly creating and sharing a false post via Facebook on April 6 last year with the intent to annoy others.
The post was viewed on April 7 at around 5pm at the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) Tower 1 in Cyberjaya.
The charge was framed under Section 233(1)(a) of the Communications and Multimedia Act 1998, which carries a maximum fine of RM500,000, a jail term of up to two years, or both upon conviction.
In his judgment, Ahmad Fuad said the offence could spark negative public perception, especially since the false statement was accompanied by a photo of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, misleading the public into believing it originated from the prime minister himself.
"Be more careful on social media. Every post can be seen by thousands before going viral. Do not share such content. Stop when it reaches you. Otherwise, you bear the sin and the punishment," the judge said.
MCMC prosecution officer Nazrul Nizam Mohd Zameri handled the prosecution, while Ariffaizal was represented by counsel Shahidah Muslimah Roslan.
Earlier, the accused pleaded for a lighter sentence, citing his gout condition and his family commitments, which include a wife, an 80-year-old ailing father, and nine school-going children.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Social media users in Malaysia must verify age with using IC or passport from June 1, says deputy minister
Article:
KUCHING, May 22 — Users of social media platforms will need to upload official government-issued documents for age verification following the implementation of two new codes under the Online Safety Act (ONSA) introduced by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), effective June 1.
Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching said under the two codes, namely the Children’s Protection Code (CPC) and the Risk Mitigation Code (RMC), documents that may be used include identity cards and passports to prevent users under the age of 16 from opening social media accounts.
“We have now asked platforms to implement age verification. Users need to verify their age using government-issued documents such as identity cards, passports or other official documents.
“If it is merely self-declared, anyone can simply click and claim they are above 18 years old,” she told reporters after officiating the Safe Internet Campaign Community Carnival here today.
Teo said MCMC, however, had not prescribed any specific technology that platforms must use for the age verification process.
According to her, the government will provide a reasonable period for social media platforms to implement the age verification process for existing accounts before enforcement action is taken.
“For existing accounts that have yet to complete age verification, MCMC is still discussing a reasonable timeframe after engaging with the involved social media platforms, including TikTok, Facebook and Instagram.
“Those who fail to complete verification may have their accounts closed, or the social media platforms may need to use artificial intelligence to ensure users are aged 16 and above,” she said.
Teo said the government had previously decided that children under the age of 16 were not allowed to open or own social media accounts on platforms with more than eight million users in Malaysia.
She said the two codes were published following engagement sessions with industry players, civil society organisations and stakeholders conducted since the beginning of this year.
Earlier, in her speech, Teo said that from January to April this year alone, a total of 23,367 online scam cases were reported involving losses amounting to RM680.3 million.
She said that since the Safe Internet Campaign was launched in January 2025 until April 30 this year, more than 11,000 programmes had been carried out nationwide involving over 1.8 million participants comprising school students, youths, parents, educators, senior citizens and local communities. — Bernama

Title: Johor police arrest six men over fatal slashing of 22‑year‑old in Pekan Nanas
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 22 — Johor police have arrested six local men to assist investigations into the murder of a 22-year-old man believed to have been slashed to death in Pekan Nanas, Pontian yesterday.
Acting Johor police chief DCP Hoo Chuan Huat said the suspects were arrested in an operation conducted from midnight until early this morning at several locations around Johor Bahru.
“Investigations are ongoing and further details on the suspects will be disclosed later as the arrests were only made today,” he told a press conference at the Iskandar Puteri district police headquarters here today.
The victim was found dead in a pool of blood in front of a food outlet in Pekan Nanas, with severe injuries to his hands and legs.
In a separate case, police arrested seven foreign men aged between 17 and 45 to assist investigations into the murder of a technician at an apartment in Prima Tebrau, Johor Bahru on May 13.
Hoo said the suspects were detained over the past three days in Masai and Mersing, while police also recovered the victim’s car during one of the raids.
“All suspects have been remanded for seven days until May 26 under Section 302 of the Penal Code.
“Police confirmed the 33-year-old victim had stab wounds and investigations are ongoing to determine the motive,” he said.
On May 13, the technician was found dead after he was believed to have been stabbed multiple times in an apartment unit in Prima Tebrau. A knife believed to have been used in the incident was also found beside the victim. — Bernama

Title: Higher Education Ministry: UPUOnline offers places to more than 169,000 SPM leavers for 2026 intake
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — A total of 169,803 students who sat for the 2025 Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examination have been offered places at public higher education institutions (IPTA) through UPUOnline.
The Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) said of the total, 91,357 candidates received offers from public universities, 60,947 from polytechnics, 15,844 from community colleges and 1,655 from institutions under Majlis Amanah Rakyat (MARA) Higher Education Division.
In a statement today, MOHE said it received 235,480 eligible applications from SPM school leavers through UPUOnline this year.
“A total of 363 programmes were offered for this intake, comprising 41 certificate programmes, 57 foundation programmes, 264 diploma programmes and one bachelor’s degree programme.
“Admissions were based on the meritocracy principle, with eligible candidates ranked according to merit scores from highest to lowest,” read the statement.
The ministry also provided special pathways for 701 persons with disabilities, 310 Orang Asli candidates, 179 athletes, 10,499 candidates from the B40 group and 65 candidates from institutions under the Social Welfare Department (JKM).
“For the first time, all academic programmes are open to persons with disabilities based on their qualifications and suitability, as part of efforts to make higher education more inclusive and accessible,” the statement said.
The ministry also offered places to students who obtained excellent results of 8As and above, reflecting the government’s commitment to supporting outstanding students in pursuing higher education.
Candidates who did not receive offers may submit appeals through UPUOnline from today until 5 pm on May 31, 2026.
MOHE also congratulated successful candidates and encouraged them to make full use of the opportunity to further their studies. — Bernama

Title: Johor police bust drug lab, seize RM16.4m worth of narcotic‑laced vape cartridges
Article:
ISKANDAR PUTERI, May 22 — Police busted an illegal drug processing lab that specialised in producing narcotic‑laced vape cartridges worth RM16.4 million were seized in three raids around Skudai and northern Johor Bahru recently.
The raids carried out by the Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (CID) on May 18 saw the arrest of four men, including the mastermind and chemist of the syndicate. Those arrested were aged 27 to 60.
Acting Johor police chief Hoo Chuan Huat said those arrested were two locals and two foreigners.
“Police raided premises in Taman Melawati and Taman Ungku Tun Aminah in Skudai that were used as a drug processing laboratory.
“The third raid was carried out at a house in Taman Mutiara Emas near Skudai where a local man, believed to be the syndicate’s mastermind, was arrested,” he told reporters at the Iskandar Puteri district police headquarters here today.
Hoo said other than being the mastermind, the fourth suspect was later identified as the syndicate’s operations financier.
“The second and third suspects were the syndicate’s chemists, responsible for processing chemicals into drugs.
“The first suspect acted as the syndicate’s runner,” he said.
Hoo said that the total seizure was 94.76 litres of liquid methamphetamine and 14.61 kilogrammes of chemicals with an estimated value of RM16.40 million.
Acting Johor police chief Hoo Chuan Huat (centre) with the seized narcotics at the Iskandar Puteri district police headquarters May 22, 2026 — Picture by Ben Tan
He added that the drugs seized were estimated to be able to supply 120,649 addicts.
“The syndicate is believed to have been active since early January this year.
“Their modus operandi was to use shophouse premises as a drug processing laboratory and a place to process vape cartridges, before distributing them to the local market,” he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 39B of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952 and Section 30(3) of the Poisons Act 1952.
Hoo said drug screening tests found two suspects testing positive for methamphetamine abuse, while the other two suspects tested negative for any narcotics.
“The suspects were remanded for seven days from May 19 to 25. A check of past records found that two suspects had previous criminal offences,” he said, adding that police had also seized three Mercedes-Benz cars, a Honda Accord and a Volkswagen car, as well as RM49,400 in cash and S$9,770 (RM30,292).
Hoo said the estimated total value of the seizures amounted to RM16.58 million, making the bust the biggest success in Johor for this year.

Title: Only one new abandoned project recorded as delayed, sick private housing cases decline, says deputy housing minister
Article:
PUTRAJAYA, May 22 — Statistics on private housing projects categorised as ‘delayed’ and ‘sick’ in Peninsular Malaysia showed a declining trend, as of April 30 this year, compared with March, said Deputy Housing and Local Government Minister Datuk Aiman Athirah Sabu.
She said a total of 130 projects were recorded under the ‘delayed’ category, and 302 under the ‘sick’ category, while only one new project was classified as ‘abandoned’.
“This positive development proves that the proactive and integrated approach undertaken is beginning to yield more organised and focused results,” she said, in a statement after chairing the third 2026 meeting of the Task Force on Sick and Abandoned Private Housing Projects (TFST), today.
She added that, since its establishment in 2023, TFST has successfully revived 1,576 private housing projects, involving 185,525 housing units, with an estimated gross development value (GDV) of RM148.21 billion.
Of the total, 131 projects were successfully restored from ‘sick’ to ‘active’ status, 1,410 ‘sick’ projects were fully completed and obtained the Certificate of Completion and Compliance (CCC), and 35 ‘abandoned’ projects were successfully revived, either through physical completion of the projects or via buyer settlement plans.
She said the Ministry of Housing and Local Government (KPKT) will not compromise with any party which neglects the rights of homebuyers.
“Our focus is not merely on restoring physical structures, but on restoring the dreams and welfare of the people who have long been affected by issues involving sick housing projects,” she said.
To maintain the positive momentum, she said KPKT is also determined to gradually reduce the statistics of problematic developments, while realising the target of achieving zero abandoned projects by 2030.
The monitoring framework will also continue to be strengthened, alongside targeted early interventions, to fully safeguard and protect the rights of homebuyers in the country. — Bernama

Title: Fuziah reminds PKR MPs to abide by party decisions, uphold mandate
Article:
KUANTAN, May 22 — PKR MPs have been reminded to remain committed to the party’s mandate in carrying out their duties as elected representatives.
PKR secretary-general Datuk Dr Fuziah Salleh said every elected representative must abide by party decisions and uphold the principles of the struggle entrusted to them.
“They are not only serving their constituencies, but also representing the party and should uphold those principles, not promote other parties,” she told reporters after launching the Madani Mart branch at Jalan Dato’ Wong Ah Jang in the Teruntum state constituency here today.
Fuziah, who is also Deputy Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister, was commenting on the participation of several MPs in programmes organised by leaders of other parties.
She said several party leaders had been tasked with engaging and advising the MPs involved on their responsibilities in carrying the party’s mandate.
“This is advice from the leadership to them. So far, we respect them as MPs… they are not only serving the people, but their role as party representatives is equally important. In Parliament, when it comes to voting and other matters, they must adhere to what has been decided by the party,” she said. — Bernama

Title: Domestic Trade Ministry enforces nine‑day Kaamatan‑Gawai price‑control scheme in Sabah, Sarawak and Labuan
Article:
RANAU, May 22 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) will implement the Festive Season Maximum Price Scheme (SHMMP) in conjunction with the Kaamatan Festival and Gawai Day 2026 from May 27 to June 4.
Its Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said the scheme involves a total of 12 types of goods for Sabah and Sarawak as well as 11 types of goods for the Federal Territory of Labuan.
“The implementation of the nine-day scheme takes into account the interests of consumers and traders to ensure that essential goods can be obtained at reasonable prices without burdening the business community,” he told reporters after inspecting the RAHMAH Madani Sales Programme (PJRM) in Kundasang, near here today.
Armizan said for the 2026 Kaamatan Festival SHMMP in Sabah, a total of 12 types of goods are listed compared to 10 types last year with the addition of imported ginger and potatoes from China.
“The items involved are live pigs, pork (meat and fat), pork (belly), imported buffalo meat from India, chicken wings, curly dried chillies, small shallots from India, garlic from China, large yellow onions, large shallots, imported aged ginger and potatoes from China.
“For Labuan, 11 types of items are listed, namely pork (meat and fat), pork (belly), imported buffalo meat from India, chicken wings, curly dried chillies, small shallots from India, garlic from China, large yellow onions, large shallots, imported aged ginger and potatoes from China,” he said.
He said a comparison of the implementation of the Kaamatan Festival SHMMP for the years 2025 and 2026 showed a more stable price trend in Sabah and Labuan when six items recorded a price decrease, while several other items remained at their current prices.
He said for the 2026 Gawai Day SHMMP in Sarawak, 12 types of items were listed compared to 11 last year’s type with the addition of buffalo meat imported from India.
“Among the items involved are live pigs, pork (meat and fat), pork (belly), live old chicken, chicken wings, imported round cabbage, garlic from China, dried curly chili, coconut seeds, grated coconut and coconut milk,” he added.
Armizan said the SHMMP is enforced under the Price Control and Anti-Profiteering Act 2011 [Act 723] and allows for the setting of maximum prices for the items involved.
“KPDN will place enforcement officers in strategic locations such as public markets, farmers’ markets, Tamu markets and shopping centres to ensure compliance with the scheme,” he said.
He said traders who sell price-controlled items above the maximum price can be fined up to RM100,000 or imprisoned not more than three years for individuals, while companies can be fined up to RM500,000.
“For the offence of failing to put up pink price tags for controlled items, individuals can be fined up to RM10,000 and companies up to RM20,000,” he said. — Bernama

Title: LLM forecasts 3.3 million vehicles on highways amid Aidiladha, school holidays
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 22 — The Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) expects the average daily traffic volume on major expressways and connecting highways to reach 3.3 million vehicles on peak days in conjunction with Hari Raya Aidiladha, which will be celebrated on May 27.
LLM said in a statement that traffic flow is expected to increase progressively from today until June 7 due to the overlap between the festive season, public holidays and the extended school holidays.
It said the PLUS highway network is projected to record the highest traffic volume at 2.2 million vehicles, followed by the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) with 283,000 vehicles and the Kuala Lumpur-Karak Expressway (KLK) with 209,000 vehicles.
“To ensure safer and smoother journeys for road users throughout the festive period, LLM has instructed all highway concessionaires to implement several proactive measures, including prohibiting lane closures except for emergency works from May 23 to June 2,” it said.
LLM would also be activating 34 Smart Lane locations, including along the PLUS highway network, while ensuring that toll collection systems function efficiently, including staggered toll collection at critical toll plazas.
Operational inspections of highways and facilities would also be conducted at Rest and Service Areas (R&R) and lay-bys, provision of 65 portable toilets, as well as additional parking spaces and personnel, including RELA members, at affected locations.
LLM advised road users to plan their journeys using the TuJu application and Travel Time Advisory (TTA), ensure their vehicles are in good condition and maintain sufficient balance in their Touch ’n Go accounts, e-wallets and debit cards before starting their journey.
In addition, motorcyclists are reminded to exercise extra caution and make use of the 571 motorcycle shelters provided in the event of bad weather.
Road users can also obtain real-time traffic and incident updates through LLM’s official channels and related applications. — Bernama

Title: Man gets 30 years, 9 strokes for incest with now-pregnant underage niece in Kanowit
Article:
SIBU, May 22 — The Sessions Court here sentenced a 23-year-old man to a total of 30 years’ jail and nine strokes of the cane for committing incest with his 14-year-old niece.
Judge Musyiri Peet imposed a sentence of 10 years’ jail and three strokes of the cane for each of the three charges under Section 376B(1) of the Penal Code, and ordered the imprisonment to run consecutively.
The accused was charged with committing incest with his niece on three occasions, namely at around 2am on June 1, 2025 and around 11pm on June 2, 2025, and at around 8.30am on February 13, 2026.
All three offences took place in a room of a house in Kanowit.
Based on the facts of the case, a doctor at Kanowit Hospital examined a 14-year-old female patient at around 4.30pm on May 11, 2026 and found that she was 31 weeks pregnant.
During an initial interview, the teenager revealed that she had had sexual relations with the accused, who is her uncle.
Acting on a report lodged, police arrested the accused at around 7.30pm the same day.
Deputy public prosecutor Cynthia Emmelda Jerry prosecuted the case, while the accused was unrepresented by legal counsel. — The Borneo Post
